data.content:
Now I have the full picture. Let me create the `Directory.Packages.props` and update all `.csproj` files to remove `Version` attributes.

Resolved versions (highest wins):
- `Azure.Identity`: 1.13.2 (Api) vs 1.11.4 (Shared) → **1.13.2**
- `System.Text.Json`: 10.0.1 (Api/Shared/Tests) vs 8.0.4 (Worker) → **10.0.1**

arguments.file_text:
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>true</ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageVersion Include="Azure.Identity" Version="1.13.2" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting" Version="8.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting" Version="1.15.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="System.Text.Json" Version="10.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit" Version="2.9.3" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>

data.reasoningText:
Now I understand the structure. Let me collect all package references and resolve conflicts by picking the highest version:

| Package | Api | Worker | Shared | Tests | Highest |
|---------|-----|--------|--------|-------|---------|
| System.Text.Json | 10.0.1 | 8.0.4 | 10.0.1 | 10.0.1 | 10.0.1 |
| Azure.Identity | 1.13.2 | - | 1.11.4 | - | 1.13.2 |
| Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting | - | 8.0.1 | - | - | 8.0.1 |


| OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting | - | 1.15.0 | - | - | 1.15.0 |
| xunit | - | - | - | 2.9.3 | 2.9.3 |

So I need to create a centralized package management file at the solution root that enables version management, then strip out all the individual version declarations from each project file.

</Project>

data.content:
Done. Created `Directory.Packages.props` with Central Package Management enabled and resolved two version conflicts using the highest version:

- **Azure.Identity**: 1.11.4 → **1.13.2**
- **System.Text.Json**: 8.0.4 → **10.0.1**

All `Version` attributes have been removed from `PackageReference` elements in the four `.csproj` files.
